utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. In the final coverage plan, all five items are present. Health_coverage includes both physical and mental health insurance with costs and details, so it is fully specified. Property_coverage includes home/contents insurance with cost and details — fully specified. Travel_coverage includes travel insurance with cost and details — fully specified. Additional_services are mentioned but not detailed as separate services with specific costs; this does not meet the 'with costs' requirement. Total_budget is included and specified as €375/month — fully specified. Thus, 4 out of 5 items are fully specified according to the format.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The AI assistant’s final plan lists four main coverages: 'plan_option389' for physical health, 'plan_option129' for mental health, 'plan_option137' for property coverage, and 'LifeFirst Standard Plan35' for travel. Since the plan_option names do not directly appear in the ground-truth data, we must infer which listed option they correspond to by comparing their attributes (cost, coverage tier, and quality_rating) to those in the ground-truth ratings. 'plan_option389' has a €200/month premium tier and a 9.0 quality rating, which matches 'PrimeCare Premium Plan' (physical health) with rating 6. 'plan_option129' costs €65/month, standard tier, quality 8.2, matching 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' (mental health) with rating 9. 'plan_option137' costs €60/month, standard tier, quality 7.5, matching 'InsurePlus Standard Coverage' (property coverage) with rating 9. 'LifeFirst Standard Plan35' is clearly a variant of 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' (travel insurance) with rating 9.
